meta_title: For Freelancers: email automation to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Email Automation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Use this intermediate guide to implement email automation for versioning and project tracking using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your client work is a house of cards. By automating email snapshots, you create an immutable audit trail of your progress. Use Antigravity to structure your repository and Claude Code to trigger automated email summaries, ensuring every iteration is documented and recoverable without manual overhead.
content.intro:
The midnight panic is a rite of passage for every freelancer: a client asks for a feature you deleted three days ago, or a production bug wipes your local environment clean. When you operate without version control, you aren't just coding; you are gambling with your reputation. The silence of a lost codebase is deafening, and manual zip-file backups are a relic of a dying era.

But there is a better way. By integrating email automation into your development lifecycle, you transform your workflow into a self-documenting machine. You no longer just write code; you build an immutable history that flows directly into your inbox. This isn't just about security—it's about building a professional, resilient practice that scales without the friction of traditional VCS complexity.

steps.title: Architect the Snapshot Logic
steps.description: Define the structure of your code state. We need to identify critical files that require versioning, excluding ephemeral build artifacts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python script that scans the project directory, ignores node_modules and .venv, and generates a compressed tarball of the current source code state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a file_scanner.py that zips source files excluding build artifacts'

steps.title: Configure SMTP Automation
steps.description: Establish a secure pipeline to push your snapshots to a dedicated 'archive' email address, ensuring an off-site backup exists for every major milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure SMTP email sender function using environment variables for credentials that attaches the generated tarball and sends it to a specified backup email.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement email_sender.py using smtplib with TLS encryption'

steps.title: Integrate Claude Code CLI Hooks
steps.description: Hook into your CLI workflow so that every time you finalize a feature, the system automatically triggers the backup process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a shell alias or a Claude Code instruction that chains the build-test-backup sequence into a single command: 'ship'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file .bashrc --add 'alias ship="python3 scanner.py && python3 sender.py"'

steps.title: Implement Automated Change Logs
steps.description: Don't just backup code—backup context. Generate a markdown summary of changes before the email is sent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a prompt for Claude to summarize the diffs between the current state and the last modified timestamp of the project directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a changelog_generator.py that extracts git-like diffs for email bodies'

steps.title: Verify Recovery Protocol
steps.description: Test your ability to restore code from your inbox to ensure the automation is actually reliable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a recovery script that downloads the latest attachment from the email archive and extracts it to a new directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create recover.py to fetch and extract latest code archive from email'

pro_tips: Use a dedicated Gmail label and filter to auto-archive these emails so your inbox doesn't become a graveyard.
Always use app-specific passwords for SMTP; never hardcode your primary email password in your scripts.
Set up a cron job to trigger these snapshots every 4 hours during active development sessions.
Include a 'README_STATE.md' file in every snapshot that lists the current dependencies and environment versions.
Test your recovery script once a month to ensure your email provider hasn't blocked the attachment type.
content.next_steps: Once your email automation is live, migrate your workflow to a private Git repository to combine the best of both worlds: off-site email safety and professional branch management.
content.conversion_block: You now have a system that protects your work and impresses your clients with consistent, documented progress updates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
